mission.title: Install and Configure Kube Vip on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kube-Vip provides a highly available virtual IP and load-balancer for Kubernetes control planes and services. Installing Kube-Vip simplifies the setup of HA Kubernetes clusters, making it suitable for various environments including bare-metal and edge devices.

steps.title: Add the Kube-Vip Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kube-Vip Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kube-vip https://kube-vip.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kube-Vip using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kube-Vip in the `kube-vip` namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install kube-vip kube-vip/kube-vip --namespace kube-vip --create-namespace --version v1.0.4
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kube-Vip pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip
```

steps.title: Expose Kube-Vip service
steps.description:
To access the Kube-Vip service, you can port-forward it to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kube-vip -n kube-vip 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kube-Vip will show the Kube-Vip pods in the `kube-vip` namespace as running. You will also be able to access the service via port-forwarding on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kube-vip https://kube-vip.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install kube-vip kube-vip/kube-vip --namespace kube-vip --create-namespace --version v1.0.4

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kube-Vip Helm release from the cluster using the following command. This will remove the deployment and associated resources from the specified namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall kube-vip --namespace kube-vip
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources are cleaned up properly.
```bash
kubectl delete crd kubevips.kube-vip.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `kube-vip` namespace to remove any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-vip
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it is important to back up the current state of the Kube-Vip deployment. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get deployment kube-vip -n kube-vip -o yaml > kube-vip-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts, then upgrade the Kube-Vip installation to the desired version (e.g., v1.1.0).
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kube-vip kube-vip/kube-vip --namespace kube-vip --version v1.1.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the Kube-Vip pods to ensure they are running the new version and functioning correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kube-Vip pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for any errors that may indicate the cause of the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-vip
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kube-Vip service is not reachable, verify that the service is correctly configured and that the pods are running. Check the service details and pod status.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kube-vip
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources allocated
troubleshooting.description:
If Kube-Vip is failing to start due to insufficient resources, check the resource limits and requests set in the deployment. Adjust them if necessary.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment kube-vip -n kube-vip
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration errors
troubleshooting.description:
If there are issues with the configuration, review the deployment configuration and logs to identify any misconfigurations.
```bash
kubectl get deployment kube-vip -n kube-vip -o yaml
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-vip
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
